name: K-Means Clustering
applicable_scenarios: partition continuous-feature data into k convex clusters
customer / user segmentation on engineered features
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

def kmeans_fit(X, k, seed=0):
    model = KMeans(n_clusters=k, n_init='auto', random_state=seed)
    labels = model.fit_predict(X)
    return model, labels
common_pitfalls: requires choosing k in advance; use elbow / silhouette / gap statistic
Euclidean distance assumes isotropic clusters; scale features first
k-means++ init matters — plain random init is unstable
cannot discover non-convex clusters; consider DBSCAN or spectral
